data.title: Trump says he's raising South Korean tariffs on certain goods to 25%
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is boosting tariffs on South Korean autos, lumber, pharmaceuticals and other goods to 25% from 15%, he said on Monday.

Trump cited South Korea's failure to ratify a trade deal he said was finalized with President Lee Jae Myung last year. 

“Because the Korean Legislature hasn’t enacted our Historic Trade Agreement, which is their prerogative, I am hereby increasing South Korean TARIFFS on Autos, Lumber, Pharma, and all other Reciprocal TARIFFS, from 15% to 25%,” Trump said in a post on his social media platform Truth Social.

It was reported earlier in the month that South Korea will look for favorable terms for U.S. tariffs on imports of memory chips.

Related tickers: (EWY), (KORU), (FLKR).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Osisko Development launches $125 million bought-deal share offering
data.description:
• Osisko Development (ODV) on Monday said that it has agreed to a bought-deal public offering of about 35.3 million common shares at a price of $3.54 per share, for gross proceeds of about $125 million.
  • The company said the proceeds will be used to fund infill and exploration drilling at its Cariboo Gold Project and for general working capital purposes.
  • The underwriters have an option to buy up to an additional 5.3 million shares to cover over-allotments, the company said.
  • ODV -1.81% after hours to $3.78.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Team Inc names Gary Hill as CEO, reaffirms 2025 outlook
data.description:
• Team (TISI) on Monday said that Chief Executive Officer Keith Tucker will retire after more than 20 years with the company, and that its board has appointed Gary Hill as chief executive officer effective February 1, 2026.
  • Hill most recently served as Chief Operating Officer of Shermco Industries and has previously held senior leadership roles at AIS Holdings and AZZ Inc, the company said.
  • Team also reaffirmed its previously issued 2025 outlook.
  • TISI -0.0% after hours to $14.0.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Humana, other insurers fall on flat rates for Medicaid Advantage payments in 2027
data.description:
• Humana (HUM), UnitedHealth Group (UNH), and several other insurers are down in after-hours trading Monday after the Trump administration proposed nearly flat reimbursement rates for Medicare Advantage payers in 2027.
  • The average payment increase is slated at 0.09%, or ~$700M overall. Street analysts were anticipating a 4%-6% rise.
  • However, the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services noted: "When considering estimated risk score trend in MA driven by coding practices and population changes, the expected average change in payments will be 2.54%.
  • CVS Health (CVS), Molina (MOH), and Elevance Health (ELV) are other insurers on the decline in after-hours trading.

data.title: Vale mine overflow in Brazil sends water into competitor CSN's site
data.description:
Vale (VALE) closed -2.9% on Monday after saying it is investigating an overflow of water with sediment that occurred in a pit at its Fábrica mine in Brazil's Minas Gerais state during the weekend.

Miner and steelmaker Companhia Siderurgica Nacional (SID) said tailing water flooded its facilities near the mine, including a loading area; CSN said its mining containment structures are operating normally, but it is not yet known if the incident will affect production.

Vale (VALE) said its own output would not be impacted by the incident.

The municipal government of Congonhas said Vale's (VALE) slurry reached streams in the town, and a second overflow occurred at the company's nearby Viga mine.

The incident does not involve a dam, and all dams in the region remain stable and under continuous monitoring, Vale (VALE) said; the Brumadinho dam collapse in Minas Gerais seven years ago killed 270 people.

data.title: Brown & Brown GAAP EPS of $0.93 beats by $0.29, revenue of $1.6B misses by $50M
data.description:
• Brown & Brown press release (BRO): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.93 beats by $0.29.
  • Revenue of $1.6B (+35.6% Y/Y) misses by $50M.
  • Income before income taxes of $321 million, increasing 16.7%, with Income Before Income Taxes Margin of 20.0%, compared to 23.2% in the fourth quarter of the prior year.
  • EBITDAC - Adjusted of $529 million, increasing 35.6%, with EBITDAC Margin - Adjusted of 32.9%, compared to 32.9% in the fourth quarter of the prior year.
  • Net income attributable to the Company of $264 million, increasing $54 million, or 25.7%, compared to the fourth quarter of the prior year.

data.title: Freddie Mac mortgage portfolio increases 1.9% in January
data.description:
Freddie Mac's (FMCC) mortgage portfolio increased at an annualized rate of 1.9%  in January to $3.59T, slower growth than its 4.8% increase in December, according to the company's monthly volume summary released on Monday.

Purchases of mortgage-related investments in January 2026 amounted to $23.8B compared with $32.5B in December and $14.2B of purchases in January 2025.

Recall that early in January, President Donald Trump directed the government's representatives (i.e., FMCC and FNMA) to acquire $200B in mortgage bonds to narrow mortgage spreads and lower borrowing costs. However, it was unclear as to when those purchases would occur.

Freddie Mac (FMCC) mortgage-related securities and other mortgage-related guarantees increased at an annualized rate of 3.2% in January.

The single-family delinquency rate increased to 0.61% in January from 0.59% in December, while its multifamily delinquency rate rose to 0.42% from 0.40%.

data.title: First Bank GAAP EPS of $0.49 in-line, revenue of $38.46M beats by $1.04M
data.description:
• First Bank press release (FRBA): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.49 in-line.
  • Revenue of $38.46M (+13.9% Y/Y) beats by $1.04M.

data.title: Crane shares dip after hours as sluggish orders temper Q4 results
data.description:
Crane (CR) shares fell about 1% in extended trading Monday after the company reported fourth-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations but flagged softer demand in part of its portfolio and a 2026 outlook that brackets, but does not clearly top, consensus forecasts.

The Stamford, Conn.-based manufacturer posted adjusted earnings of $1.53 a share from continuing operations for the fourth quarter, ahead of the Wall Street consensus estimate of $1.41 a share. Revenue rose to $581 million, above analyst expectations of $572 million.

Net income rose to about $82 million, or $1.39 a share, from roughly $71 million, or $1.20 a share, a year earlier.

Fourth-quarter sales increased 6.8%, driven by 5.4% core growth, modest acquisition contributions and favorable foreign exchange. Operating profit climbed 18.1% to $101.8 million, reflecting higher productivity and pricing.

M&A margin pressure

Investors appeared to focus on several cautionary signals in the report. Core orders grew just 2.4% in the quarter, while management said orders in the process flow technologies segment remained sluggish. That unit posted a 1.5% core sales decline and saw backlog fall to $359.9 million at year-end from $383.0 million three months earlier.

Crane (CR) also indicated that recent acquisitions will pressure margins in the near term. The company said its 2026 adjusted segment operating margin is expected to be about 22.5% or higher, compared with a comparable 23.7% in 2025, citing initial dilution from acquisitions. In addition, higher interest expense tied to acquisition financing is expected to lift net non-operating expense to about $58 million next year.

2026 guidance

For 2026, Crane (CR) initiated adjusted earnings guidance of $6.55 to $6.75 a share, reflecting about 10% growth at the midpoint. The outlook compares with the current Wall Street estimate of $6.67 a share and implies limited upside without stronger order momentum or faster synergy realization.

Chief Executive Officer Max Mitchell said in a statement that the quarter capped “another outstanding year,” highlighting record sales and profit in the company’s growth segments.

Crane (CR) ended the year with $506.5 million in cash and $1.15 billion in debt following the closing of multiple acquisitions. Management said the acquisition contribution in 2026 will be largely offset by interest expense, with more meaningful earnings benefits expected in 2027 and beyond.

data.title: Abbott Laboratories snaps six straight sessions of losses
data.description:
Abbott Laboratories (ABT) shares snapped six straight sessions of losses, as the stock closed 1.2% higher at $108.77 on Monday.

The MedTech giant started its downward trend on Jan. 15. The stock has lost over 13% in the preceding six sessions.

ABT closed 1% lower on Friday at $107.42. January has been a less favorable month for the company, as it traded 12 sessions in red and just three sessions in green.

Last week, the Illinois-based company’s shares closed 10% lower on Thursday after it reported lower-than-expected revenue for Q4 2025 following underperformance in its Nutrition segment.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, ABT has a Hold rating with a score of 3.06 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it got D- in the growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 21 analysts gave ABT a Buy and above, eight analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and no one recommended Sell or lower. Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and see the stock as a Buy.

“The recent pullback in Abbott Laboratories appears to be driven more by short-term sentiment than by any deterioration in its long-term fundamentals. Near-term weakness in the Nutrition and Diagnostics segments has pressured results. However, ABT continues to deliver solid earnings growth, expanding margins, and strong performance in high-growth areas,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis by Gen Alpha.

Another Seeking Alpha analysts also argued that a post-earnings sell-off and a price dip are buying opportunities for investors.

Earlier in the day, Abbott said its Chairman and CEO Robert Ford purchased 18.8K of the company’s stock recently with a value of just over $2M.

The stock rose 10% last year, compared to the 16% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.
